item.title: Petrochemicals Giant Braskem Seeks Support to Start Out-of-Court Rework
item.description: Braskem SA is seeking backing from creditors to begin an out-of-court restructuring process ahead of debt payments scheduled for July, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Microsoft’s New Quantum Chip Aiming for Useful Machine in 2029
item.description: Microsoft Corp. announced an updated quantum-computing chip, betting it can set up a commercially useful machine by 2029.

item.title: Microsoft Launches AI That Works Like an Executive Assistant
item.description: Microsoft Corp. launched new artificial intelligence software designed to function like an always-active executive assistant, the latest evolution of its workplace AI efforts.

item.title: Microsoft’s Project Solara Aims to Put AI in an Employee Badge
item.description: Microsoft Corp. introduced a new set of software tools designed to take artificial intelligence agents off of laptop screens and place them in mobile devices for businesses.

item.title: 12 Reasons This Is The Worst Crypto Winter Ever
item.description: It’s getting colder and colder.

item.title: Indie Video Game ‘Mina the Hollower’ Sells 300,000 Copies in Three Days
item.description: The new action-platformer is the best-reviewed video game of 2026 so far

item.title: Canadian Stocks Index Hits 35,000 Amid Rally in Energy
item.description: The Canadian equities benchmark passed 35,000 for the first time on a broadening rally in energy and financials.

item.title: Uber Caps Usage of AI Tools Like Claude Code to Cut Costs
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. has set usage caps on some artificial intelligence-powered tools used by its staff, a move meant to manage costs after the company blew through its AI budget earlier this year.

item.title: Perplexity Splits AI Work Between PCs and Servers to Ease Strain
item.description: Perplexity AI Inc. is trying to manage the huge demand for artificial intelligence computing power by building a platform that diverts AI work between personal computers and cloud-based servers.

item.title: EU’s AI Data Center Plans Stumble Due to Delays, Funding Issues
item.description: The European Union’s €20 billion ($23.3 billion) investment plan for five massive artificial intelligence data centers is floundering, with delays and funding issues alienating some potential partners, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Alphabet $80 Billion Equity Sale Set to Break Fundraising Record
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s surprise $80 billion sale of equity to fund spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure is expected to become the biggest equity capital markets transaction of all time, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

item.title: Santander Employee Arrested in $85 Million Chile Crime Sting
item.description: An employee of Banco Santander’s Chile unit was among 18 suspects detained by police Tuesday as authorities moved against the Venezuelan Tren de Aragua criminal organization allegedly operating in the South American country.

item.title: SpaceX Staffers Prep for Multimillion-Dollar Windfalls by Pushing for VIP Terms
item.description: The workers are crafting an IPO wealth playbook that could shape how OpenAI and Anthropic employees cash in.

item.title: Bitcoin’s Inflation-Hedging Promise in Tatters After 36% Plunge
item.description: Bitcoin has fallen 36% over the past year and slipped below $70,000 this week, extending a retreat that is undermining several of the arguments that helped carry the cryptocurrency into the financial mainstream.

item.title: Venezuela Wants Oil Firms to Supply Their Own Power for Projects
item.description: Energy companies heading to Venezuela are being told to bring their own power plants to run their oil and natural gas operations and shield them from frequent blackouts on the nation’s ill-maintained electricity grid.

item.title: Rheinmetall Wins Romanian Defense Contracts Worth €5.7 Billion
item.description: Rheinmetall AG, Germany’s largest defense company, will provide €5.7 billion ($6.7 billion) worth of military gear to Romania to help shore up NATO’s Eastern flank, the firm’s biggest international contract package since the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: OpenAI Plans AI Tools for Finance, Legal in Race With Anthropic
item.description: OpenAI is expanding the functionality of its artificial intelligence coding agent for a number of professions beyond software engineering, a bid to compete with rivals such as Anthropic PBC in signing up more business customers.

item.title: BOE’s Greene Says Hikes Are on the Cards as Iran War Drags On
item.description: Bank of England rate-setter Megan Greene said the case for interest rate hikes is strengthening as the Iran conflict continues, a sign that some officials could join the central bank’s chief economist in voting for tighter policy.

item.title: Chipmakers Outperform Software Stocks by Most on Record
item.description: Semiconductor stocks rallied on Tuesday while software fell, with the gap between the two sub-sectors of the tech industry the widest on record.

item.title: Blue Owl Tech Private Credit Fund Is Selling $300 Million Bonds
item.description: A private credit fund managed by Blue Owl Capital Inc. is looking to sell $300 million of investment-grade bonds, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: UAE’s Strait of Hormuz Pipeline Bypass May Also Include Fuels
item.description: The United Arab Emirates is considering building an additional pipeline for refined products that would bypass the Strait of Hormuz and carry fuels to the country’s east coast.

item.title: Brightline Fields Bankruptcy-Loan Bids While Hoping for a Suitor
item.description: Brightline is weighing bankruptcy-loan offers from its largest creditors after the Florida passenger railroad failed to lure a buyer by a self-imposed bidding deadline last month, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz Traffic Remains Thin Amid Peace-Deal Uncertainty
item.description: Commercial vessel traffic through the crucial Strait of Hormuz appeared to remain limited over the past day, amid uncertainty over prospects for a US-Iran peace deal.

item.title: Meta’s Rosen, Former Head of Election Integrity, to Depart
item.description: Guy Rosen, Meta Platforms Inc.’s chief information security officer who previously oversaw the company’s election integrity work, is departing in the coming months.

item.title: Denmark’s New Government Plans to Cut Corporate, Income Taxes
item.description: Denmark’s new center-left government said it will cut the corporate tax rate to shore up the Nordic country’s ability to compete in a tougher global environment marked by trade wars and soaring energy costs.

item.title: Vitol Says Europe and US Aren’t Facing Up to Oil Supply Crunch
item.description: Vitol’s top executive in the Middle East said that many Western governments still aren’t reckoning with the oil supply crunch that’s rippling around the world due to the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Nestlé CEO Says Lower Coffee, Cocoa Costs Will Boost Margins
item.description: Nestlé SA Chief Executive Officer Philipp Navratil said lower coffee and cocoa prices should help improve margins this year as the foodmaker works to reignite growth.

item.title: Blue Origin’s Lengthy Launchpad Repair Extends SpaceX Dominance
item.description: Blue Origin faces a formidable challenge that goes beyond simply replacing the New Glenn rocket it lost in last week’s explosion: rebuilding a destroyed launchpad that took years to construct.

item.title: Carney Says Data Will Be ‘Uneven’ as Canada Dips Into Technical Recession
item.description: Canadian economic data will be volatile as the government works on a broader transformation of the economy, Prime Minister Mark Carney said in response to last week’s gross domestic product report that showed the country tipped into a technical recession.

item.title: Large Prepaid Energy Deals Poised to Hit Booming Muni Sector
item.description: The muni market is slated to see a prepaid electricity deal amounting to as much as $1.25 billion that will add to the onslaught of supply in the surging market segment.
